title: Open Philanthropy Effective Giving Theory of Change

nodes.title: Program Funding
nodes.text: $15-20 million annually for effective giving and careers initiatives

connections.evidence: Clear budget allocation and existing grantee relationships. OpenPhil has sustained funding track record.
connections.assumptions: Funding will be sustained long-term through economic cycles. Effective grantee selection continues. Grantees use funds effectively for intended purposes.

nodes.title: Program Staff
nodes.text: Dedicated program officers specializing in meta-interventions

connections.evidence: Direct operational control with dedicated specialized staff.
connections.assumptions: Staff expertise sufficient for effective outreach. Target audiences reachable and responsive. Outreach methods remain effective over time.

connections.evidence: Program officers have research background and institutional support.
connections.assumptions: Staff capacity adequate for research alongside operational duties. Research findings will be actionable and influential.

connections.evidence: Program staff have expertise to create educational materials and direct operational control over content development.
connections.assumptions: Staff have time and skills for content creation alongside other duties. Educational content remains current and high-quality over time.

nodes.title: Grantee Organizations
nodes.text: Partnerships with effective giving organizations like GiveWell, Giving What We Can

connections.evidence: Established partnerships with proven effective giving organizations. Track record of successful collaboration.
connections.assumptions: Grantee organizations maintain effectiveness and alignment. Community-building activities translate to sustained engagement.

connections.evidence: Grantee organizations like GiveWell and GWWC already produce substantial educational content as core activities.
connections.assumptions: Grantee organizations will create content aligned with OpenPhil's goals. Content quality and messaging remain consistent across organizations.

nodes.title: Network Access
nodes.text: Connections to high-net-worth individuals, professionals, and career changers

connections.evidence: OpenPhil's reputation and existing networks provide access to target demographics.
connections.assumptions: Network access translates to engagement. High-net-worth individuals open to evidence-based giving approaches.

connections.evidence: Network connections provide potential donor pipeline.
connections.assumptions: Personal networks can be systematically cultivated for effective giving. Wealthy individuals responsive to peer influence.

nodes.title: Research Infrastructure
nodes.text: Analysis systems for giving patterns, career impact, and movement research

connections.evidence: Existing infrastructure and methodological expertise for impact assessment.
connections.assumptions: Research infrastructure can scale with program growth. Analysis methods accurately capture real-world impact patterns.

nodes.title: Funded Organizations
nodes.text: Sustained support for effective giving organizations and career guidance platforms

connections.evidence: Grantee organizations like GiveWell have demonstrated track record of influencing donations.
connections.assumptions: Funded organizations can effectively convert their resources into additional donations. Marginal funding increases organizational impact rather than displacing existing funding.

nodes.title: Individual Outreach
nodes.text: Programs reaching potential effective givers and career changers

connections.evidence: 80,000 Hours data shows career guidance can influence career choices, though impact varies widely.
connections.assumptions: Individual outreach translates to actual career changes. Career advice recipients make optimal use of guidance. High-impact careers remain available for career changers.

connections.evidence: Some evidence from giving pledges and GiveWell donors, but attribution challenging.
connections.assumptions: Outreach reaches people who wouldn't otherwise donate effectively. Giving behavior changes persist over time.

nodes.title: Educational Content
nodes.text: Resources on effective giving principles and high-impact career paths

connections.evidence: Educational content has reach metrics but behavior change attribution is limited.
connections.assumptions: Content reaches target audiences effectively. Educational material translates to belief and behavior changes. Content quality maintains over scaled production.

nodes.title: Research Publications
nodes.text: Analysis of giving patterns, career impact, and movement growth

connections.evidence: Academic and policy research has mixed track record of institutional influence. EA research has limited mainstream penetration.
connections.assumptions: Institutional decision-makers read and are influenced by research. Research findings support effectiveness-focused approaches. Academic credibility translates to policy influence.

nodes.title: Community Building
nodes.text: Networks and platforms connecting effective altruists and career changers

connections.evidence: EA community has grown substantially with clear community-building activities driving engagement.
connections.assumptions: Community growth translates to increased impact rather than just social activity. Community maintains quality and effectiveness focus while scaling.

nodes.title: Donor Cultivation
nodes.text: Targeted outreach to high-capacity potential effective givers

connections.evidence: Some success stories in major donor cultivation, though systematic evidence limited.
connections.assumptions: High-net-worth individuals responsive to cultivation efforts. Personal relationships translate to sustained giving commitments. Cultivation scales without diminishing returns.

nodes.title: Increased Effective Donations
nodes.text: Significant growth in donations to highly effective charities

connections.evidence: GiveWell and other EA organizations have tracked substantial money moved. Some documented cases of high leverage ratios.
connections.assumptions: Additional donations represent truly marginal impact rather than displacing other giving. Donated money is used effectively by recipient organizations.

nodes.title: Career Impact Multiplication
nodes.text: More professionals working in or supporting high-impact sectors

connections.evidence: Some career transitions documented but systematic impact measurement challenging. Long-term outcomes uncertain.
connections.assumptions: Career changes generate higher impact than previous career paths. High-impact sectors can absorb additional talent effectively. Career advice recipients optimize their impact within chosen paths.

nodes.title: Movement Growth
nodes.text: Expansion of the effective altruism and effective giving movements

connections.evidence: EA movement has grown but mainstream cultural influence remains limited. Some indication of broader awareness.
connections.assumptions: Movement growth translates to broader cultural influence. Growing movement maintains effectiveness focus rather than diluting principles.

nodes.title: Institutional Adoption
nodes.text: Organizations and foundations adopting effectiveness-focused giving approaches

connections.evidence: Limited examples of major institutional adoption. Most philanthropic institutions remain traditional in approach.
connections.assumptions: Institutional changes spread through sector influence. Organizations implement effectiveness approaches authentically rather than superficially. Institutional adoption survives leadership changes.

nodes.title: Cultural Shift
nodes.text: Broader acceptance of impact-focused giving and career choices

connections.evidence: Cultural change in philanthropy historically slow. Limited mainstream adoption of EA principles to date.
connections.assumptions: Cultural shifts can be accelerated through targeted interventions. Broader culture will adopt effectiveness focus without losing other important values. Cultural change translates to resource allocation changes.

nodes.title: Leverage Effects
nodes.text: Each dollar spent generating 5-50x in additional effective giving and career impact

connections.evidence: Some documented cases of high leverage ratios, though systematic measurement challenging. Historical precedent for meta-interventions scaling.
connections.assumptions: Leverage effects compound rather than diminish over time. High leverage ratios can be maintained at scale. Meta-interventions don't hit saturation points.

nodes.title: Self-sustaining Ecosystem
nodes.text: Effective giving and high-impact careers become mainstream, directing billions toward pressing problems
